error.signature: cv2.error: (-28:Insufficient number of valid points) in function 'calibrateCamera'

verdict.summary: Camera calibration fails because too few calibration images had detected corners. Need more images or better pattern detection.

dead_ends.action: Reduce the number of required corners to match what was detected
dead_ends.why_fails: Specifying fewer corners than the actual pattern causes wrong correspondences and bad calibration.

dead_ends.action: Use low-resolution images to make corner detection faster
dead_ends.why_fails: Lower resolution reduces corner detection accuracy and subpixel refinement quality.

workarounds.action: Capture more calibration images (at least 15-20) at varied angles
workarounds.how: Tilt board at 30-45 degrees. Cover all regions of the frame. Vary distance from camera.

workarounds.action: Verify findChessboardCorners succeeds before adding to calibration set
workarounds.how:
ret, corners = cv2.findChessboardCorners(gray, (9, 6))
if ret:
    objpoints.append(objp)
    imgpoints.append(corners)

gray = cv2.equalizeHist(gray)  # improve contrast
cv2.findChessboardCorners(gray, (9,6), cv2.CALIB_CB_ADAPTIVE_THRESH)
